title: Misleading variable name l2_payload_len is actually a slice, not length
description:
# Problem
In `rust/lichen-node/src/stack.rs:290-297`:

```rust
let l2_payload_len = wrap_schc_payload(&schc[..schc_len], &mut l2_payload)?;

let wire_len = self
    .link
    .build_frame(self.epoch, seqnum, &[], l2_payload_len, &mut wire)
```

The variable `l2_payload_len` is a `&[u8]` slice (return type of `wrap_schc_payload`), but the name strongly suggests it's a `usize` length. The actual behavior is correct, but a contributor reading this code will assume it's a length.

## What goes wrong
1. A contributor copying this pattern might write `build_frame(..., payload.len(), ...)` instead of `build_frame(..., payload, ...)`
2. Such a bug would type-check (usize coerces in some contexts) and cause silent data corruption
3. Code reviewers familiar with the codebase will skip over this because they know the pattern, missing bugs from contributors

## Fix
Rename to `l2_payload` or `l2_payload_slice` to match its actual type.
close_reason: Renamed l2_payload_len to l2_payload in stack.rs (line 294-302, 320-327)
